title: New task auto assigned to creator of automation not the person who triggered it

posts.author: vIQleS (Nathan Grange)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’ve got an automation that creates a new task in a different board when an item’s status is changed:

image1901×456 58.3 KB

On that board, I’ve got an automation that assigns the reporter based on who created the new item:

image1858×349 38.1 KB

But every item that’s created by the automation is being attributed to me. (Because I created the Automation? :-/)

Obviously what I want is for the reporter to be the person who triggered the automation (usually a dev).

posts.author: engledc (Duane Engle)
posts.cookedHtml: I am having this same issue. Is there a fix?

posts.author: ivanhong-monday.com (ivan-monday.com)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @vIQleS and @engledc!

Thanks so much for writing in and sharing this valuable feedback with us. At the moment, I’m afraid that there isn’t a fix just yet regarding the system attributing the person that created the automation as the one that created the item.

This is definitely something that is on our radar and I’d love to pass this additional feedback along to our Autopilot Team. I can see the impact of being able to have the person that triggered the recipe be the person that is shown as the creator and I’m hopeful that this can be added as a feature addition!

posts.author: jnc (James Nonu-Carling)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi there,

This same issue is still happening. Is there a fix on the horizon?

Thank you

posts.author: aaragon (Amanda A)
posts.cookedHtml: Chiming in on this as well, as it’s two years later and this is still happening. Any fix planned?

posts.author: Maureen-Ke (Maureen Njue)
posts.cookedHtml: Would be great if this is looked into by Monday. Abit impractical to register the Automation owner and not the person who actually created the item

posts.author: kswenar (Karen)
posts.cookedHtml:
We’re running into this issue too, and it’s made it really tough to get accurate analytics on who’s actually entering the items.

Has anyone found a good workaround or temporary fix until Monday can roll out a solution?

posts.author: rt-ops (Andy)
posts.cookedHtml: Looks like this is STILL an issue. Becoming increasingly disappointed with Monday and 1.the number of these illogical little problems 2.the lack of any progress addressing them (apparently 5 years on this one for example!)

posts.author: MassielGonzalez (Massiel Gonzalez)
posts.cookedHtml: This is still an issue, and I would love a solution to this, please.
